'One more thing,' Velvet adds quietly. 'If we do this and fail, Prism won't just kill us. She'll harvest us. Our memories, our personalities, everything we are — fed into the stack network and distributed as raw material. We won't just die. We'll be erased.'

choices.text: Recruit Councilor Graves — he has Inner Circle access.
Contact the Ghost Syndicate for outside support.
Approach Chrome directly. The bodyguard has been acting strange.

nc_075.title: The Power Play
text: Velvet stares at you. 'You want to use this to... take over the Court?' She's quiet for a moment, then a slow, complicated smile crosses her face. 'That's either brilliant or insane. Possibly both.'

'Think about it,' you say. 'If we expose Prism's transfer method to the rest of the Court — not the public, just the Court — it undermines her authority. The Courtiers, the Associates, even some of the Inner Circle might not know the full truth. They think the host bodies are volunteers or clones. If they learn Prism is murdering people to stay alive...'

'Some will be horrified. Some won't care. But it creates instability. And in instability, there's opportunity.' Velvet nods slowly. 'We'd need to control the narrative. Present ourselves as the ethical alternative. Build a coalition before Prism can retaliate.'

'And we'd need to deal with Prism herself. She's two hundred years old, she's survived a dozen coup attempts, and she has Chrome. This isn't a chess game — it's a knife fight in the dark against someone who's been fighting in the dark since before either of us was born.'

choices.text: Build the coalition. Start with Councilor Graves.
Go directly to Prism. Confront her with what you know.

nc_076.title: The Timeline
text: 'Three days,' Velvet says. 'The transfer prep takes seventy-two hours. Asha is already in the final stages — consciousness thinning, personality being stripped layer by layer to make room for Prism's engram. By the time of the transfer, Asha's mind will be a blank slate. There's nothing left to save if we wait too long.'

'Prism's Inner Circle is gathered for the event. Dahlia is managing security logistics. Graves is providing medical oversight — he hates it, but refusing would end his position. Vex is handling the post-transfer celebration. Chrome, as always, guards the door.'
She meets your eyes. 'Three days. Whatever we're going to do, we need to decide now and act fast. This is the moment, one way or another. The Court pivots on what happens in that room on Floor 199.'
Three days. One choice that determines the fate of the Court, of Asha, and of everything you've built since that gilded invitation slipped under your door.

choices.text: Plan a rescue for Asha.
Plan a coup against Lady Prism.
Do nothing. Let the transfer happen and maintain your position.

nc_077.title: Facing the Court
text: You go to Velvet. She's waiting in the same trial chamber where you passed the Trial of Thorns — the symmetry isn't lost on you. 'I've bought you a window,' she says, 'but it's closing. Prism suspects you but hasn't confirmed it. If you can provide a plausible alternative source for the leak, you might survive this.'

The investigation is led by Dahlia, who enjoys it with visible relish. She interrogates you for three hours, probing for inconsistencies, watching your micro-expressions. It's the hardest conversation of your life.
You need to either convince Dahlia of your innocence or deflect suspicion onto someone else.

choices.text: Blame the leak on a fabricated external source.
Admit to the leak and challenge Prism directly.

nc_078.title: The Escape
text: You run. The Court's tracking implant burns in your arm as you descend through the tower's service corridors, but you prepared for this — a black-market signal jammer purchased weeks ago, just in case. It buys you maybe six hours before they recalibrate.

By the time Chrome arrives at your quarters, you're already in the Lower District, buried in the crowds and chaos of Sector 7. The Ashen Circle provides safe harbor — Sister Cinder opens a door and pulls you inside without a word.

You've lost everything: the quarters, the credits, the wardrobe, the power. But you've kept your life and your evidence. From the Lower District, you watch the Harvest scandal unfold. The Court is damaged but not destroyed. Prism is still in power. The revolution you hoped to spark is a slow burn, not an explosion.
But the fire is lit. And you're free to fan it.

choices.text: Join the revolution against the Court from the outside.

nc_079.title: Graves's Decision
text: Councilor Graves listens to your plan with the expression of a man who's been waiting for this conversation for years. 'I've known about the host erasure since my first transfer oversight,' he says quietly. 'I've hated it. I've rationalized it. I've lost sleep over it. And I've done nothing, because doing something meant dying.'

He paces his lab, hands clasped behind his back. 'But my synthetic engram research is nearly complete. If we can stop this transfer and prove that synthetics are viable, we can end the Harvest and the host erasure in one move. Prism wouldn't need stolen bodies anymore.'

'I'll help. I have Inner Circle access. I can get you into Floor 199. But I need a guarantee: if this works, the Court transitions to synthetic technology. No more harvesting. No more murder. That's my price.'
You shake hands. Three days. The clock is ticking.

choices.text: Prepare the rescue operation.

nc_080.title: Syndicate Backup
text: You reach out to your Ghost Syndicate contacts — Whisper, if the partnership was established, or cold contacts if not. The Syndicate is intrigued. Anything that destabilizes the Neon Court creates opportunities for them.

'We can provide a diversion,' the Syndicate operative says. 'A coordinated attack on three Court-owned nightclubs simultaneously. Every enforcer and security team in the tower will be pulled to respond. That gives you maybe thirty minutes with reduced security on the upper floors.'
'In return, we want the Court's memory synthesis formulas. The tech that makes their designer drugs. If the Court falls or weakens, we want to be ready to fill the vacuum.'
It's a steep price — handing Ghost Syndicate the keys to one of the Court's most profitable operations. But you need the diversion.

choices.text: Accept the deal. The diversion is essential.
Reject the price. Find another way to create a diversion.

nc_081.title: Chrome's Secret
text: You find Chrome in the tower's training facility — a vast space on Floor 180 where the bodyguard practices combat routines with mechanical precision. No one else is present. The facility is silent except for the sound of Chrome's movements through the air.
You speak to Chrome directly, knowing the bodyguard has never responded to anyone except Lady Prism. 'I know about Asha. I know about the transfer. I know what happens to the hosts.'
Chrome stops mid-routine. The mirror visor turns toward you. For a long, terrible moment, nothing happens.
Then Chrome reaches up and removes the visor.

The face underneath is young — impossibly young for someone who's served as Prism's bodyguard for decades. And the eyes... the eyes are full of a grief so deep it has become architecture, a structure built to contain pain.

'I was the first,' Chrome says. A woman's voice, raw from years of silence. 'The first host body. Prism used me, then moved on. But the procedure wasn't perfect back then. Part of me survived the erasure. Not enough to be who I was. Enough to remember what she did.'
'I've been waiting for someone inside the Court who would help me stop her. I'll get you into Floor 199. I'll deal with Prism myself.'

choices.text: Accept Chrome's help. Prepare the rescue.

nc_082.title: Building the Coalition
text: You and Velvet spend two days in a frenzy of quiet politics. Graves joins immediately when approached — his opposition to the Harvest makes him a natural ally. Lord Vex is harder. He's a survivor, not an idealist, and his loyalty is to whoever holds power.

You present Vex with the truth about Prism's transfers and a simple argument: 'If Prism can erase anyone's consciousness and take their body, none of us are safe. Today it's volunteers from the Lower District. Tomorrow it might be one of us.'

Vex goes pale. The implications land. 'I'll support a change of leadership,' he says carefully, 'if — and only if — the transition is managed. No chaos. No vacuum. The Court's operations must continue. My operations must continue.'

Dahlia remains loyal to Prism. Chrome is an unknown quantity. The coalition is fragile — Graves for moral reasons, Vex for self-preservation, you and Velvet for something in between. But it's enough to move.

choices.text: Launch the coup during Prism's transfer ceremony.

nc_083.title: Confronting Prism
text: You request a private audience with Lady Prism. She grants it, which either means she doesn't suspect you or she's confident enough in her power that suspicion doesn't matter. You meet in her chambers on Floor 200 — alone, except for Chrome standing motionless by the door.
'I know about the host bodies,' you say. No preamble. No games. 'Asha. And everyone before her. You don't transfer consciousness to willing vessels. You erase people and move in.'

Prism's expression doesn't change. She studies you the way she studied you at your first meeting — like a collector evaluating an acquisition. 'Yes,' she says simply. 'I do. I have for two hundred years. Does that shock you?'

'The bodies I've worn were poor, desperate, damaged people with no futures. I gave their flesh purpose. I gave their existence meaning it never would have had otherwise. Is that murder? Or is it the ultimate form of recycling?' She smiles. 'You climbed very high, very fast, to arrive at this confrontation. What do you intend to do with it?'

choices.text: "End the Harvest. Adopt synthetic technology. Or I expose everything."
"Step down. The Court needs new leadership."
"Teach me. I want to understand immortality from your perspective."

nc_084.title: The Silent Witness
text: You do nothing. You tell yourself it's strategic patience, but the truth is simpler and uglier: you've been inside the machine too long. The luxury, the power, the insulation from consequences — it's changed your calculus. Asha is a stranger. Your position is everything you've worked for.

You attend the transfer ceremony as a witness. Floor 199 is prepared like an operating theater crossed with a temple — clinical equipment surrounded by ceremonial lighting and flowers. Asha lies on the central platform, sedated, her consciousness already thinned to a whisper. Lady Prism reclines on an adjacent platform, serene.

The procedure takes four hours. You watch a two-hundred-year-old mind pour itself into a twenty-year-old body. Asha's face changes — subtly at first, then dramatically. New expressions, new light in the eyes, new movements. By the end, Asha is gone. Prism wears her body like a new coat, stretching, testing, smiling.
'Young again,' Prism says, flexing new hands. She looks at you. 'You stayed. You watched. You chose comfort over conscience. That makes you one of us.' The words feel like a door closing.

choices.text: Accept your place. Rise to Inner Circle.
The guilt is too much. Plan to bring the Court down from within.

nc_085.title: The Deflection
text: Your performance is masterful. You construct a narrative involving a disgruntled former clinic technician — someone who exists in the Court's records but left three months ago under unfavorable circumstances. You plant just enough detail to make the story credible and just enough doubt about your own involvement to make investigating you seem like a waste of resources.

Dahlia isn't entirely convinced, but she's pragmatic. Chasing the fabricated lead is faster than interrogating a Courtier with political connections. The investigation shifts targets. You breathe again.

Velvet is impressed and troubled. 'You lie very well,' she observes. 'I'm not sure if that's a compliment or a concern.' But the immediate danger passes. The Harvest scandal continues to burn in the public sphere, weakening the Court's reputation while you remain inside, protected, positioned.
The question now is what to do with that position.

choices.text: Use the chaos to push for reform from within.

nc_086.title: Caught
text: Dahlia sees through you. Maybe your tells are too obvious, maybe she's simply better at this game than you are. 'You're lying,' she says flatly. 'Chrome — detain the Courtier.'
Chrome moves toward you. Velvet steps between you and the bodyguard, a move that costs her everything. 'If you touch them, I walk. And I take my entire network of contacts with me.'
The standoff lasts three heartbeats. Then Prism's voice comes through the chamber's speakers: 'Enough. Bring them both to me. This is no longer Dahlia's investigation. It's mine.'
You and Velvet are escorted to Floor 200. Whatever happens next, you face it together.

choices.text: Face Lady Prism's judgment.

nc_087.title: Prism's Judgment
text: Lady Prism sits in a chair that's more throne than furniture, watching you with the patience of someone who's had centuries to learn it. Chrome stands behind her. Velvet is beside you, defiant but afraid.

'You leaked the Harvest data,' Prism says. Not a question. 'You exposed the Court's most sensitive operation to public scrutiny. You've cost us contracts, clients, political capital, and at least three Inner Circle members' reputations.'

She stands and walks toward you. 'The old me would have you harvested. Fed into the stack network piece by piece until nothing was left. But I'm curious — you rose faster than anyone in decades. You showed brilliance, adaptability, and a moral center that I find simultaneously infuriating and compelling.'

'So I'm giving you a choice. A real one, not the kind the Court usually offers.' Her ancient eyes bore into yours. 'Join my Inner Circle. Help me reform the Harvest from within — slower than you'd like, but permanently. Or leave the Court and take your revolution to the streets, where it will burn bright and change nothing.'
'What do you choose?'

choices.text: Accept. Join the Inner Circle and reform from within.
Refuse. Leave the Court and fight from outside.
"Neither. I'm taking the Court from you. Tonight."

nc_088.title: The Night of Prisms
text: It happens on the night of the transfer ceremony. Whether through careful planning or desperate improvisation, all the pieces converge on Floor 199 of Cloud Tower Seven.

The diversion hits first — Ghost Syndicate strikes, or a manufactured crisis, or simply the chaos of the Harvest scandal reaching critical mass. Court enforcers scramble. The tower's security grid lights up with alerts. In the confusion, you move.

Velvet is with you. Graves provides Inner Circle access codes. Chrome — if allied — leads the way, the tower's most dangerous guardian now turned against its master. If Chrome isn't with you, you fight through two security checkpoints that leave you battered but moving.

Floor 199. The medical suite. Asha on the platform, consciousness flickering like a candle in wind. Prism on the adjacent platform, eyes closed, ready for transfer. Dahlia at the controls. And then you walk in.

choices.text: Stop the transfer. Save Asha at any cost.
Confront Prism. Demand her surrender and a peaceful transition of power.
Redirect the transfer — put Prism's consciousness into a synthetic host instead.

nc_089.title: The Ultimatum
text: You face Lady Prism across the room that has been her seat of power for decades. Behind you, your allies — however many you've gathered. Before you, two centuries of accumulated power, cunning, and ruthlessness.
'The Harvest ends,' you say. 'The host erasure ends. The Court transitions to synthetic memory technology. And you step down from leadership.'

Prism laughs. Not cruelly — genuinely, with the amusement of someone who's seen this before. 'Step down? My dear, I am the Court. Without me, this entire structure — the clinics, the clubs, the political networks, the stack technology itself — collapses. You're not threatening a leader. You're threatening a civilization.'
She's not entirely wrong. But she's not entirely right either. The Court's power comes from systems, not just one consciousness. And systems can survive changes at the top.
'Last chance,' you say.

choices.text: Follow through. End Prism's reign.
Negotiate — Prism reforms, but keeps a reduced role.

nc_090.title: Prism's Response
text: Prism's amusement fades. 'Step down,' she repeats, tasting the words like a new flavor. 'No one has said that to me in... oh, sixty years? Seventy? The last one who tried is currently a decorative installation in the Gallery. Lovely expression, frozen permanently in surprise.'
'You have conviction. I admire that. But conviction without power is just noise.' She signals, and Chrome steps forward. Or should. Chrome doesn't move.
The bodyguard stands perfectly still, visor reflecting the room. Prism turns to look, and for the first time in perhaps decades, uncertainty crosses her face. 'Chrome?'
Whatever happens next depends on the alliances you've built and the choices you've made.

choices.text: Press the advantage while Prism is uncertain.

nc_091.title: The Temptation of Eternity
text: Prism's expression shifts — from guarded amusement to something like genuine interest. 'You want to understand. Not judge, not fight, not reform. Understand.' She sits back, considering you with fresh eyes.

'Two hundred and twelve years,' she says softly. 'I was born in the old world, before the towers, before the stacks, before any of this. I watched the world rebuild itself. I helped build it. And I realized that the greatest tragedy of human existence isn't suffering — it's that we learn just enough to be wise, and then we die before we can use that wisdom.'

'The bodies I've worn... yes, they were people. People who would have lived fifty, sixty years and been forgotten. I remember every one of them. Their memories, their feelings, their last thoughts before I moved in. I carry them all. Is that murder? Or is it the closest thing to true remembrance?'

She extends her hand. 'I can give you this. Immortality. Not the commercial stack transfer — the real thing. My process. You would live for centuries, wearing bodies like coats, accumulating wisdom, power, experience beyond imagination. All it costs is... well. You know what it costs.'

choices.text: Accept. Become immortal. Become the next Prism.
Refuse. Some costs are too high.

nc_092.title: The Fall of Prism
text: It ends quickly, when it ends. Prism has survived two centuries through intelligence and adaptability, but she's never faced a coalition attacking from inside and outside simultaneously. Graves locks the transfer equipment. Chrome — if allied — physically restrains Prism. If Chrome isn't with you, Velvet disables the security grid while you confront Prism directly.

Dahlia fights for Prism to the last, but Lord Vex switches sides at the critical moment, as you knew he would. The Marchioness is restrained by her own guards, snarling promises of revenge that echo off the walls.

Prism, held in place or simply defeated, looks at you with those ancient eyes. 'You'll regret this,' she says calmly. 'Not because I'll punish you. Because you'll discover that leading the Court is harder than judging it. Every compromise I made, you'll make. Every body I wore, you'll understand why. Give it time.'
Asha is saved — pulled from the transfer platform before the procedure could complete, her consciousness battered but intact. She won't remember what almost happened to her. That might be a mercy.

choices.text: Take leadership of the Neon Court. Reform it from the top.
Dismantle the Court entirely. No one should have this power.
Install Graves as leader with a reform mandate.

nc_093.title: The Synthetic Solution
text: In a stroke of desperate brilliance, you modify the transfer equipment — rerouting Prism's consciousness not into Asha's body but into a synthetic host that Graves has been developing. The prototype isn't perfect, but it's functional. A body without a mind to erase. A vessel without a victim.

The transfer initiates before Prism can object. Her consciousness streams out of her dying body and into the synthetic — a jarring, imperfect process that leaves the ancient mind disoriented and weakened but alive. Asha is disconnected from the equipment, consciousness intact.
Prism opens new eyes — synthetic eyes, in a synthetic face. She looks at her artificial hands with an expression of horror and wonder. 'What have you done?'
'Given you immortality without the murder,' you reply. 'The Court's technology just evolved. Welcome to the future.'

The Inner Circle watches. Graves is elated. Vex is calculating. Dahlia is furious. And Chrome — Chrome is weeping behind the visor, silently, watching the monster that consumed her original self trapped in a body made of circuits and polymer instead of stolen flesh.

choices.text: Propose a new Court structure with synthetic technology at its core.

nc_094.title: Ending — The Revolutionary
text: You leave the Neon Court behind — the luxury, the power, the gilded cage. The Lower District welcomes you back with the indifferent embrace of a world that never noticed you left. But you're not the same person who received that violet-edged invitation. You have knowledge, connections, and a burning purpose.

The Ashen Circle becomes your base of operations. Sister Cinder introduces you to a network of resistance cells that have been fighting the Court's influence for years. With your insider knowledge, their operations become sharper, more targeted. Harvest clinics are exposed and shut down. Court operatives are identified and turned. The slow work of revolution continues.

Velvet sends encrypted messages sometimes. She's still inside, still playing the game, feeding you information when she can. The Court weakens under sustained pressure from within and without. It may take years, but the machine is breaking down.

On clear nights, you look up at Cloud Tower Seven and remember what it felt like to live above the clouds. Then you look at the people around you — real, unmodified, struggling, alive — and you remember why you came back down.
The revolution isn't glamorous. But it's honest. And it's yours.

nc_095.title: Ending — The New Court
text: You take the throne. Not Prism's literal chair — you have that removed, replaced with a round table — but the mantle of leadership. The Neon Court under your rule is a different beast: still powerful, still decadent, still the city's social and technological elite, but with new rules and new limits.

The Harvest is abolished. Graves's synthetic memory technology becomes the Court's new foundation — stack transfers without victims, immortality without murder. It's less efficient, less vivid, and some of the old guard complain endlessly about the 'quality decline.' But nobody dies for someone else's extra century.

Velvet becomes your right hand — officially, this time. Chrome stays on as security chief, the visor finally removed, the woman underneath allowed to exist as herself for the first time in decades. Lord Vex manages the nightlife empire with his usual theatrical flair. Graves oversees the technology transition. Dahlia is exiled, her assets seized, her network dismantled.

Lady Prism, whether imprisoned or transferred to a synthetic body, watches from whatever cage you've built for her. 'Give it time,' she told you. And she was right — every day brings compromises, difficult choices, the grinding weight of power. But you make them differently than she did. Imperfectly, sometimes poorly, but with a conscience that you refuse to let atrophy.

The Neon Court endures. Changed, humbled, evolving. And at its center, you — the nobody from the Lower District who climbed the highest tower in the city and found, at the top, that the view is only worth having if you remember where you started.

nc_096.title: Ending — The Inner Circle
text: You join the Inner Circle — not as Prism's puppet, but as a voice of reform within the Court's highest ranks. It's the most difficult path: working alongside people who've enabled exploitation for decades, arguing for change in increments measured in years rather than revolutions.

Prism remains in power, but your presence forces compromises. The Harvest is scaled back — not eliminated, but reduced, with genuine volunteer programs supplementing the forced extractions. Graves's synthetic research receives full funding. Chrome is given a choice about their continued service for the first time ever.

Velvet rises with you, taking a seat at the council table she never expected to occupy. Together, you represent a new generation within the Court — one that remembers the Lower District, that sees the people below the clouds as people rather than resources.

It's not a revolution. It's evolution, slow and frustrating and full of compromise. Dahlia fights you at every turn. Prism watches with ancient amusement, occasionally supporting your reforms, occasionally blocking them, always calculating. The system bends but doesn't break.

Is it enough? Some nights, staring down at the storm layer from Floor 195, you're not sure. But the alternative — burning it all down — would leave nothing to rebuild. So you stay. You push. You compromise. And you hope that the small changes accumulate into something that matters.

nc_097.title: Ending — The Dissolution
text: You tear it down. All of it. The Neon Court's assets are seized and distributed. The stack clinics are opened to public oversight. The memory technology is released to the public domain. The cloud towers' exclusive upper floors are opened to refugees from the Lower District. For one chaotic, beautiful, terrifying week, the city's power structure inverts.

Lady Prism's consciousness is extracted from her body and stored — not destroyed, but archived, a two-hundred-year-old mind in digital suspension. 'You'll call for me eventually,' she says as the procedure begins. 'When you realize that power without structure is just chaos with better lighting.'

The aftermath is messy. Without the Court's infrastructure, the city's stack technology degrades. Memory clinics close. The immortality industry collapses, taking thousands of jobs with it. The Iron Collective and Ghost Syndicate rush to fill the vacuum, carving the Court's territory into competing fiefdoms that are, in some ways, worse than what came before.
But the Harvest is over. The host erasures are over. No more Lower District residents disappearing into clinics that promise hope and deliver oblivion. That's something. That's real.

Whether it's worth the cost depends on who you ask. The former Court elites, newly mortal, would say no. The Lower District families whose members were being harvested would say yes. You stand in the middle, watching the old world burn and hoping the new one is worth the ashes.

nc_098.title: Ending — True Immortality
text: You accept Prism's offer. The procedure is performed in her private chambers — just you, Prism, and the ancient technology that has sustained her through centuries. She guides you through the process with the tenderness of a parent teaching a child to walk.

Your consciousness is copied, refined, and prepared for transfer. A host body is selected — another Lower District resident, another life to be overwritten. You tell yourself this one is a volunteer. Prism assures you they always are. You don't look too closely at the paperwork.

The transfer is instantaneous and eternal. One moment you're in your old body; the next, you're someone else. Younger, stronger, with decades of life ahead. Your original body is discarded — cremated, recycled, forgotten. You wear a new face in the mirror and slowly, disturbingly, begin to love it.

Prism takes you under her wing. You become her protégé, her successor-in-waiting, the second immortal in the Neon Court's history. Together, you rule from the cloud tops, watching generations pass below like weather patterns.

Velvet doesn't speak to you anymore. Chrome watches with those grieving eyes. The Lower District continues to burn through lives that you no longer share. You've transcended mortality. Whether you've transcended humanity is a question you stop asking after the first century, because the answer frightens you.
You live forever. It costs you everything that made living worthwhile.

nc_099.title: Ending — The Double Agent
text: You play every side. Court member to the elite, informant to the Ashen Circle, business partner to the Ghost Syndicate, strategic contact for the Iron Collective. You become the node through which information flows — the one person in the city who talks to everyone and belongs to no one.

It's exhausting, exhilarating, and ultimately lonely. Velvet suspects but can't prove anything. Chrome watches but doesn't act. Lady Prism either doesn't know or doesn't care, which amounts to the same thing when you're dealing with a two-hundred-year-old mind.

The information you broker prevents wars, exposes corruption, saves lives, and destroys them. You're responsible for the Iron Collective raiding two Harvest clinics, saving dozens of victims. You're also responsible for tipping the Court about an Ashen Circle safe house, resulting in arrests that you feel in your chest like broken ribs.

Every faction thinks you're loyal to them. None of them are right. You're loyal to the balance — the fragile equilibrium between powers that prevents any single faction from dominating completely. It's not justice. It's not revolution. It's maintenance, performed by someone who knows all the machines intimately enough to keep them from grinding each other to pieces.

When you die — and you will die, because you refused Prism's immortality offer — the balance will shift. But while you live, you hold the center. And the center, however unstable, however compromised, is the only place where all the city's people have a chance.

nc_100.title: Ending — Velvet's Dawn
text: In the end, it's not about the Court. It's about Velvet.

When the dust settles — after the power struggles, the revelations, the choices that cut like glass — you find her in her quarters on Floor 160, watching lightning paint the clouds below. The color-shifting eyes cycle slowly: amber, violet, silver, amber. She's tired. You both are.

'We could leave,' she says, not looking at you. 'Both of us. Walk away from the Court, the factions, all of it. Find some forgotten corner of the world where the towers can't reach and the stack network doesn't matter. Be nobody for a while.'

You take her hand. The surveillance cameras watch but you don't care anymore. Let the Court see. Let Prism see. Let the whole gleaming, rotten, beautiful machine see two of its components choosing each other over the engine.

You leave that night. No announcement, no dramatic exit. Just two people descending Floor by Floor through the cloud layer, past the neon and the rain, past the drones and the vendors, past the life you built and the person you became. You descend until the towers are just lights above you, distant as stars.

The world below the clouds is hard and ugly and real. The air smells like rust and ozone. The streets are crowded with people who've never seen the inside of a cloud tower and never will. It's not paradise. It's not glamorous. But Velvet's hand is warm in yours, and when she smiles — really smiles, not the professional version — you see someone who's been waiting eleven years to exist outside a role.

The Court will survive without you. The factions will continue their wars. The Harvest will grind on until someone else stops it. But here, in the rain, holding the hand of someone who chose humanity over immortality, you've found something the Neon Court could never sell: a reason to be mortal.
